title: XRCE-DDS Agent entity resource exhaustion via unbounded entity creation
description: The Micro XRCE-DDS Agent allows a client to create an unbounded number of DDS entities (participants, topics, publishers, datawriters) using predictable session keys, leading to memory/CPU exhaustion and possible denial of service of the agent on UDP port 8888.

title: Zenoh router memory exhaustion through maximum-size frames
description: Eclipse Zenoh router accepts oversized frames near the UDP MTU (65535 bytes) without adequate bounds, causing reassembly buffer pressure and memory exhaustion. Oversized payload flooding can degrade the router on port 7447/7446.

title: XRCE-DDS malformed message parser robustness
description: Sending malformed or corrupted XRCE-DDS messages with invalid headers may crash or destabilize the agent parser. Fuzzing of fragmented and overlapping publication messages triggers undefined behavior.

title: Zenoh protocol fuzzing and KEEP_ALIVE flooding
description: A flood of protocol KEEP_ALIVE messages and randomized/mutated protocol frames against the Zenoh router can exhaust resources and cause denial of service.
